exercises.title: Preposition Fill-in
exercises.description:
Fill in the correct preposition:
1. The class starts ___ 9am.
2. I was born ___ 1995.
3. The cat is sleeping ___ the sofa.
4. We'll go ___ the rain stops.
5. She's been working here ___ 2020.
hints: at = specific time, in = longer period, on = surface
since = starting point, until = up to a point

solution.code:
1. at (specific time)
2. in (year)
3. on (surface)
4. until (up to a point)
5. since (starting point)

exercises.title: Conjunction Practice
exercises.description:
Combine each pair of sentences with the correct conjunction:
1. It was raining. We stayed home. (reason)
2. She is smart. She is lazy. (contrast)
3. Finish your homework. You can play. (condition)
4. He spoke French. He spoke Italian. (addition)

solution.code:
1. Because it was raining, we stayed home. / It was raining, so we stayed home.
2. She is smart, but she is lazy. / Although she is smart, she is lazy.
3. If you finish your homework, you can play. / Unless you finish your homework, you can't play.
4. He spoke both French and Italian. / He spoke French and Italian.

exercises.title: Preposition Error Correction
exercises.description:
Find and fix the preposition errors:
1. I'll call you in the evening at Monday.
2. She arrived in 3 o'clock.
3. We've been waiting since two hours.
4. He put the book in the table.
5. The meeting is on the next week.
hints: Check: is it a specific point (at), surface (on), or enclosed space (in)?

solution.code:
1. on Monday (not 'at Monday')
2. at 3 o'clock (not 'in 3 o'clock')
3. for two hours (not 'since two hours')
4. on the table (not 'in the table')
5. next week (no preposition needed)
